name: Ashtalakshmi
description: Ashtalakshmi, also spelled as Ashta Lakshmi, are the eight manifestations of Lakshmi, the Hindu goddess of wealth, prosperity, and fortune. They represent eight different forms of wealth and power. Each Lakshmi embodies a specific aspect of prosperity, and they are worshipped individually or collectively for blessings of well-being. Adi Lakshmi, the primal form, represents the eternal source of wealth. Dhanya Lakshmi bestows agricultural wealth and food security. Dhairya Lakshmi grants courage and strength to overcome obstacles. Gaja Lakshmi provides animal wealth and royalty, often depicted with elephants. Santana Lakshmi blesses devotees with progeny and family continuity. Vijaya Lakshmi bestows victory in all endeavors and success in life. Vidya Lakshmi grants knowledge, wisdom, and education. Dhana Lakshmi, the most common form, represents monetary wealth and prosperity. Worshipping Ashtalakshmi is believed to bring overall prosperity, happiness, and fulfillment to the devotees. Temples dedicated to Ashtalakshmi are found throughout India, where devotees seek their blessings for material and spiritual well-being. They are considered powerful goddesses who can bestow both worldly and spiritual riches upon their sincere devotees. Their collective worship is considered highly auspicious and beneficial.
subtitle: Eight Forms of Prosperity and Fortune

keyMyths.title: Samudra Manthan
keyMyths.description: Lakshmi emerges from the churning of the ocean, representing prosperity arising from effort.

keyMyths.title: Lakshmi's Curse
keyMyths.description: Lakshmi is sometimes depicted as being fickle, leaving those who are arrogant or unrighteous.

keyMyths.title: Worshiping Ashtalakshmi
keyMyths.description: Devotees worship Ashtalakshmi to gain blessings of wealth, health, and overall well-being.

sources.author: Kavitha Prasad
sources.work: Ashtalakshmi: Eight Manifestations of the Goddess of Wealth
